text: Meeting deadlines is a crucial skill for both work and study. Effective time management starts with breaking large projects into smaller, manageable tasks. You also need prioritization—decide which tasks are most important and tackle them first. Sometimes, you may have too much on your plate. In such cases, scope management is key: you may need to reduce the project's requirements or ask for an extension. Finally, always practice clear communication with your team or manager. Let them know early if you foresee a problem, rather than waiting until the last minute. This builds trust and helps you manage expectations.
